dsl_rule.translation_notes: Kaufman's 'run day' pattern translated using Donchian Channels: dc_lower approximates the low of the previous n days, and dc_upper approximates the high of the subsequent n days. The pattern confirms trend establishment when price stays within these bounds. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the signal confirms both uptrends (when price consolidates in upper range) and downtrends (when price consolidates in lower range). Exit conditions use reversal logic: long exits when price breaks below dc_lower, short exits when price breaks above dc_upper. Parameters n_lookback_days and n_forward_days are embedded in the Donchian Channel calculation (typically 20-period).

backtest_rule.reason: Kaufman's 'run day' pattern translated using Donchian Channels: dc_lower approximates the low of the previous n days, and dc_upper approximates the high of the subsequent n days. The pattern confirms trend establishment when price stays within these bounds. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the signal confirms both uptrends (when price consolidates in upper range) and downtrends (when price consolidates in lower range). Exit conditions use reversal logic: long exits when price breaks below dc_lower, short exits when price breaks above dc_upper. Parameters n_lookback_days and n_forward_days are embedded in the Donchian Channel calculation (typically 20-period).

source_rule_text: A run day occurs when the low of the current day is higher than the previous n days AND the high of the current day is lower than the subsequent n highs; this pattern confirms that a trend is in effect, with stronger confirmation as n increases.
